name: ContourPoint
summary: The ContourPoint class defines a point with possible chamfering information, used, for example, to define a contour plate.

constructors.name: ContourPoint
constructors.summary: Creates a new contour point instance.

constructors.name: ContourPoint
constructors.summary: Creates a new contour point using the given point and chamfer.

params.name: P
params.doc: The point (with X, Y and Z coordinates) to be used.

methods.name: CompareTo
methods.summary: Compares two points. To use binarysearch somekind of sorting should be used.

returns.doc: 0 if both are equal, -1 if this point is before, 1 if this point is after.

methods.name: Equals
methods.summary: Returns true if the current object and the given object are equal.

params.name: obj
params.doc: The object we wish to check the equality with.

returns.doc: True if the given object equals the current object.

methods.name: GetHashCode
methods.summary: Returns a hash code for the point. Notice, in extremely rare cases, you might not get the same hash code for two points even though they are considered equal! This should, however, happen only in extremely rare cases!

returns.doc: The hash code for the point.

methods.name: SetPoint
methods.summary: Sets the point's coordinates for the contour point.

methods.name: ToString.
methods.summary: Formats the point into a string, using the current culture.

returns.doc: The string that represents the point.

methods.name: ToString(CultureInfo)
methods.summary: Formats the point into a string, using the provided culture.

returns.doc: The string that represents the point.

methods.name: Translate
methods.signature: public virtual void Translate(double X, double Y, double Z)
methods.summary: Translates the point using the given vector.

methods.name: Zero
methods.summary: Zeros all the members of the point.

properties.name: Chamfer
properties.summary: The chamfer for the contour point.
